extraction.one_line_summary: GRØNEWEEGEN Dylan won a chaotic sprint with no leadouts after Gaviria lost Quick-Step's Philippe/Alaphilippe to Martin's crash group, enabling a freelance finish.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step missing Alaphilippe in crash chase fractured Gaviria's leadout, proving critical personnel loss trumps individual speed in sprint finishes.
Grøneweegen capitalized on right-side road positioning when Gaviria pinned left fence, showing lane choice matters when leadout trains disintegrate.
Martin sitting mid-peloton with 17km remaining on easy stage reveals chronic positioning weakness that cost 1:14, not crash itself.

rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: had superior speed but initiated headbutt contact with Greipel after poor positioning, revealing composure issues under chaotic sprint conditions
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: crashed 17km out positioned too far back on easy flat stage, exposing career-long tactical weakness of sitting deep to save energy
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: launched sprint from distance but quickly swamped by Greipel/Gaviria/Grøneweegen, confirming Week 1 sprint dominance already fading
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: caught behind Martin crash eliminated Quick-Step's critical leadout piece, showing sprint train vulnerability to single personnel loss
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: neutral: held barrier line legally against Gaviria contact, second place confirms late-career speed still competitive when positioned correctly
